review_title: Great PEO and outsourced HR solution for small businesses
description: Overall, we've had a good experience with TriNet. The "outsourced HR" platform TriNet offers is the right decision for our small business so we don't have to invest in internal HR personnel.
author: Phil P.
pros: TriNet helps us manage payroll, talent acquisition and retention, support for our employees, access to "big company" benefits and helps our management team with proactive risk mitigation and data compliance.
cons: TriNet is expensive and the support we received for our company 401(k) plan was not good.

review_title: User friendly software for smaller businesses with one location.
description: TriNet is very user friendly, easy to navigate and great for a small business. It is intuitive and somewhat customizable. I can't say it was a great value for money. For less costs, we could have switched to UKG. We ended up leaving them for ADP due to having multiple companies that we needed to manage that TriNet was just not capable of doing under one dashboard.
author: Amanda K.
pros: I used TriNet when it was still Zenefits. Overall, I think the software was very easy to use for payroll and employee pay, benefits administration, performance reporting and tracking, onboarding, and employee management. I will say, the program was easy to navigate, which allowed for a very minimal learning curve, which I appreciated.
cons: I would not recommend TriNet if you have multiple companies that you need to manage within one system. This made payroll management and running reports quite the ordeal. I had to run the same report, in each company profile, and then combine the data. When you're doing this for 8 companies, it can be annoying. The other thing about TriNet was the payroll compliance issues. It would take a representative FOREVER to acknowledge receipt and then even longer for an answer. I had to stay on top of them constantly.

description: Trinet was a bigger company. They didn’t want to spend time and effort for smaller start ups. Mergers are generally messy for their customers.
author: Rajesh S.
pros: Trinet was good for HR and Payroll. Working with their staff. It’s time management system was quite reliable.
cons: Trinet acquired my old vendor Zenefits then jacked up the price by 3X. Merger of the two was painful.

description: It was great. iT was a huge help since we were so new. Trinet was very supportive and walked us through each element of HR. And helped decrease our risk and liability.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Trinet was really helpful for our organization. It helped us organize everything we needed as a new organization. It helped a lot that they were able to trouble shoot any issues which took a huge weight off of us. Since we already had so much to do.
cons: We were a new organization so all the information was a bit overwhelming. It was confusing to understand how exactly Trinet worked & each segmented division.

description: We work with an excellent Account Representative who makes collaborating with TriNet easy and efficient. Communication is prompt—typically within a few hours when we need assistance—which makes a significant difference in our overall experience.
author: Jackie J.
pros: TriNet removes the burden of staying compliant with state employment laws and regulations. They manage all employer-related filings across the states where we have employees, and payroll is calculated, processed, and reported accurately without requiring hands-on involvement from our team. This automation gives us confidence that everything is handled correctly and on time.
cons: Accessing certain reports can be challenging. Some reports are not readily available within the system and must be requested directly, which is not always obvious unless you already know they exist.

review_title: TriNet is the only employee payroll system you’ll need!
description: TriNet is a holistic platform for all of our HR and payroll needs. It’s easy to use, secure, and overall reliable!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: TriNet is an extremely reliable platform. My team and I utilize this for any information and processes related to payroll and HR mangament, as well as tax forms!
cons: Nothing, TriNet has provided everything we need across the board to access our data related to payroll, tax information, and HR related data.

review_title: Good for small US subsidiaries but expensive, watch the minimums
description: TriNet served its purpose as our first US payroll solution and handled the basics competently. The low employee minimum made them accessible when we were just starting out. However, the cost became hard to justify as we grew, and their inflexibility when we temporarily dropped below minimums forced us to switch. Good starter solution for EU companies entering the US market, but be aware of the price premium and contractual requirements.
author: Sandeep S.
pros: Allowed us to run payroll for our US subsidiary starting at a low number of employees, which was critical as a German company expanding to the US. Benefits integration worked well and saved us from managing multiple vendors.
cons: Extremely expensive per employee. Software felt clunky and dated. Most critically, don't dip below their employee minimum.

description: My overall experience with using TriNet is a great one. I use this daily and the fact that I can access my pay stubs easily is a Big PLUS. I'd definitely recommend TriNet to others.
author: Meghan A.
pros: I'd have to say that the one thing I like about TriNet is how user friendly it can be and the perks that they have.
cons: There is only one small minute thing that was not a huge fan of is when i want to check my time and attendance for my hours I have to leave the app and be redirected to another one.

description: Overall it was a good system. I know companies that use and love TriNet, it just didn't work for the business needs at the time.
author: Lashelle F.
pros: TriNet had great customer service and we had a great account manager for HR related items. The system was fairly easy to use.
cons: It didn't support our international organization the way we hoped it would've. Also, the system wasn't as intuitive as you wanted it to be for certain processes.

description: TriNet was a system that I felt could've provided more value. It was mostly used for HRIS data and payroll and wasn't as great as expected.
author: Meredith R.
pros: I liked that everything was all on the home page and there was a chat feature that I used quite often.
cons: It was a clunky system with not a whole lot of helpful features. It often left me wanting more. I hope they've made more updates since I used it in 2022 and 2023.

review_title: Had high expectations from the sale and they completely failed to deliver
description: Horrible Lack of integration capabilities manual process lack of automation
author: Anthony B.
pros: The fact that they would handle payroll tax filings and the ability to help offer diverse benefits
cons: Their team does not back up what their sales people pitch at all what so ever!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Trinet has good customer service. They have been able to help me when I call in with questions. Easy to use. Secure.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's easy to use and allows me to go back in the history and pull reports of payroll. Trinet is the perfect most secure tool to use for payroll.
cons: Trinet has been easy to use. Good customer service. Trinet is also very secure so I am never worried.

author: Hussein S.
pros: Payroll processing, tax filing, benefits administration, onboarding, and employee relations all in one platform.
cons: TriNet can be expensive, especially for small businesses. Costs are typically per employee per month or a percentage of payroll, which adds up.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Sahil S.
pros: Its customer service was speedy and accurate. I loved that they always gave accurate information
cons: The interface for accessing FSA benefits. It was not very user-friendly. I found it challenging to navigate and had to call Customer support. I felt a bit lag as well.

description: Overall good experience over several years.
author: Richard K.
pros: More of a white glove level of service targeted to smaller and medium sized organizations that do not dedicated internal HR resources
cons: We had an issue relating to benefits management that led us to terminate our relationship with Trinet

description: Trinet is a scam. They have very non-transparent pricing. With all of their reports, it is next to impossible to understand what you are paying. It turns out they are ripping off their customers. My monthly service fee has more than doubled in 3 years from $500 per month to $1100 per month. My worker's compensation has increased to $1400 per month per employee when the maximum payout is $1099 per week--most companies have a maximum salary when calculating workers compensation. My medical premiums have increased from $48,000 per year per employee to $150,000 per year per employee which I believe is a direct violation of the affordable care act. Trinet has many reports and emails and alerts but you can't find these hidden prices any where. Avoid them at all costs because on top of these scam prices, their service is horrible.
author: Josh S.
pros: Over five years ago, Trinet used to save me money on health insurance and time on payroll. Now it provides lousy service at insanely high prices.
cons: Trinet is a scam. They have very non-transparent pricing. With all of their reports, it is next to impossible to understand what you are paying. It turns out they are ripping off their customers. My monthly service fee has more than doubled in 3 years from $500 per month to $1100 per month. My worker's compensation has increased to $1400 per month per employee when the maximum payout is $1099 per week--most companies have a maximum salary when calculating workers compensation. My medical premiums have increased from $48,000 per year per employee to $150,000 per year per employee which I believe is a direct violation of the affordable care act. Trinet has many reports and emails and alerts but you can't find these hidden prices any where. Avoid them at all costs because on top of these scam prices, their service is horrible.

review_title: TriNet as a Professional Employment Organization
description: TriNet is ideal if you have a small business that cannot get a good small company health insurance quote (usually less than 10 employees). However, once you get over the minimum threshold to compete in the insurance market, then TriNet is of little value.
author: Jared T.
pros: TriNet is easy to use and provides all you need from a payroll, benefit and HR perspective.
cons: The overall cost is high and the health insurance benefits are low to average quality, with fairly high deductibles and poor coverage.

review_title: Expensive for the Lack of Service
author: Garret T.
pros: I like the concept of a PEO, especially for our company that has employees in several states.
cons: The support team is less than helpful. Questions receive links to knowledge base instead of having a personal relationship with anyone at TriNet. Upon a termination of an employee we were given bad instructions which cost us $1400 in exit costs. The fees to process are expensive compared to Gusto or other similar providers. The technology platform to understand the different health options is not great. Not intuitive and no one available to help you decipher any of the information. Save yourself a headache and a pile of cash by using a different PEO.

review_title: Amazing solution but not as good as ADP
description: The overall experience has been good, but when comparing to using ADP, ADP takes the cake by a mile.
author: Beno A.
pros: The best part about Tri Net is customer support. They have the best customer service representatives that are not only knowledgeable but super friendly. The solution itself is easy to use, but the app interface can be better. The app interface needs some work, they don't even come close to ADP, but still, get the job done.
cons: I don't like their app or online interface. It is not user-friendly and you have to really look at where you are clicking or you have to start all over again from the main hub.

review_title: TriNet is definitely worth the price!
author: Rachel M.
pros: I like that it is a one stop shop for all HR services including ATS, payroll, benefits enrollment, performance management and employee onboarding.
cons: At times, customer support wasn't the quickest or the most detailed in their responses but other than that i have enjoyed the TriNet experience.

review_title: A good tool for HR designed for small companies
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use, you can access all your HR documents in a few clicks.
cons: The PTO tab is a bit hard to understand, it could be improved.

review_title: Full featured suite for payroll and employee management
description: Its was comparatively easy to setup and once its all up and running it's really easy to manage moving forward.
author: Chris W.
pros: I love that TriNet just takes care of everything for you, they really are a full featured offering that has everything you need for HR/Payroll and benefits.
cons: It's a little cumbersome to use, their integrations are very obviously just tacked on as an afterthought.

description: I used to really like TriNet and the personal service they delivered. However, they have changed their customer service model, and we are not happy with the changes they have made.
author: Chris P.
pros: I really liked the personal service TriNet used to provide. However, they have changed their Customer Service model to more of a call center instead of having two people assigned to each client.
cons: I really do not like the new Connect 360 they have recently adopted. I also find working with their reporting module somewhat challenging

description: Overall I was happy with Trinet. The main thing was that everything was accessible through the single dashboard which makes all the difference.
author: Usman K.
pros: I liked that all employee benefits were under the trinet system. I have used other systems where health stuff may be viewed from one dashboard but let's say you want to look at your commuter benefits, that may be in another system from some other company. Trinet had everything under a single website/dashboard and I only needed to remember that one login.
cons: The UI could be made better. It definitely has aa learning curve and seems very corporate and slow. It also reminds you a lot of HR.

description: Overall we're very satisfied with TriNet - from the benefits offered to the functionality of the portal and the webinars provided to educate our staff on best practices.
author: Lynn G.
pros: Communication has been superb. TriNet has created a modern platform that helps us get answers quickly both for admins and the employees. The portal is easy to use and functionality meets our needs.
cons: Cost is a little high - they are not a low cost provider but the service has been great.
